revision.text: #redirect[[en:Sebeschsen]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Infobox Ägyptische Gottheit |NAME = =S29-D58 N33*N33:V13 N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)|Mittleres Reich]] |NAME2 = =E17-D58 V13:O34 N14 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = [[Griechisch-römische Zeit|Griech.-röm. Zeit]] |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Sebches
''{{unicode|Sbẖs}}''''
lies ''Sebschesen''
''Der/Die Umschirmende'' }} '''Sebschesen''' (auch '''Sebches''') ist die Bezeichnung eines [[Altes Ägypten|altägyptischen]] [[Dekade (Altägypten)|Dekans]], der mindestens zwei [[Baktiu|Dekan-Stern]] umfasste und zum [[Sternbild]] [[Absches]] gehörte. Als auffälligster Dekanstern konnte [[Spica]] im Sternbild [[Jungfrau (Sternbild)|Jungfrau]] identifiziert werden, der zugleich als [[Stern]] der Gottheit [[Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Min]] galt und bei den jährlichen [[Prozession]]en des [[Min-Fest]]es besungen wurde. In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Sebschesen am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den achten Dekan, der in zwei Unterdekane aufgeteilt war. Der [[akronych|Eintritt in die Unterwelt]] war für den [[Schemu#Datierungen|6. Schemu I]] und der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang für den 16. Schemu III angesetzt. Als Datierungsgrundlage galt die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Siehe auch == * [[Liste der Dekan-Sterne im Nutbuch]] == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 88–89 * Christian Leitz u.a.: ''[[Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen]]; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, S. 253–256. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, S. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

In den Dekanlisten der [[Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|Sethos-Schrift]] repräsentierte Sebeschsen am Leib der [[Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|Nut]] den achten Dekan, der in zwei Unterdekane aufgeteilt war. Der [[akronych|Eintritt in die Unterwelt]] war für den [[Schemu#Datierungen|6. Schemu I]] und der [[heliakisch]]e Aufgang für den 16. Schemu III angesetzt. Als Datierungsgrundlage galt die verfügte Anordnung unter [[Sesostris III.]] ([[12. Dynastie]]) in dessen siebtem Regierungsjahr. == Literatur == * [[Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, S. 88–89 * Christian Leitz u. a.: ''[[Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen]]; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, S. 253–256. * [[Alexandra von Lieven]]: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, S. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (u. a.), Kopenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, S. 62–67 und S. 385. [[Kategorie:Dekanstern (Altägypten)]]

lies ''Sebeschsen''

{{Infobox Hieroglyphen Zweispaltig |NAME = =S29-D58 N33*N33:V13 N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|de|Mittleres Reich (Ägypten)}} |NAME2 = =E17-D58 V13:O34 N14 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit}} |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Sebeches
''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decade (Ancient Egypt)|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Absches|de|Absches}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|de|Spica}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|de|Jungfrau (Sternbild)}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)}} and was celebrated during the annual {{ill|Min festival|de|Min-Fest}} processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychal rising|de|akronych}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セベシェン]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[de:Sebeschsen]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]]

The {{ill|Acronychal rising|de|akronych}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. ''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Absches|de|Absches}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|de|Spica}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|de|Jungfrau (Sternbild)|lt=Virgo}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min}} and was celebrated during the annual {{ill|Min festival|de|Min-Fest}} processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychal rising|de|akronych}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)}}, which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu}} (decan stars) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild}} {{ill|Absches|de|Absches}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|de|Spica}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|de|Jungfrau (Sternbild)|lt=Virgo}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min}} and was celebrated during the annual {{ill|Min festival|de|Min-Fest}} processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychal rising|de|akronych}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Shemu#Dating|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[ja:セベシェン]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]][[en:draft:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]][[Category:new pages]] [[Category:Untranslated pages]] [[Category:pages needing attribution]][[Category:Sebeschsen]][[Category:pages needing attribution]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day]][[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:new pages]] [[Category:bump 1-25]] [[Category:properly attributed pages]][[Category:Sebeschsen]][[Category:properly attributed pages]]

The {{ill|Acronychal rising|de|akronych}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|de|Spica}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|de|Jungfrau (Sternbild)|lt=Virgo}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|de|Stern}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. Dynastie}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Nut|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|12=draft|12=draft|12=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{Interlanguage link|12=draft|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. ''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|qq=draft}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|de|Spica|qq=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|de|Jungfrau (Sternbild)|lt=Virgo|qq=draft}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|qq=draft}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. ''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|de|Altes Ägypten|qq=draft}} {{ill|Decan|de|Dekade (Altägypten)|1=Decan|qq=draft}}, which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft}} (decan stars) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|de|Sternbild|qq=draft}} {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|qq=draft}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|de|Spica|qq=draft}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|de|Jungfrau (Sternbild)|lt=Virgo|qq=draft}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|de|Stern|qq=draft}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|qq=draft}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=draft}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| }} {{draft categories| [[Category:Pages without meta categories]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sebschesen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sebschesen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Decan star (Ancient Egypt)]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Day link incomplete]] [[Category:Sebeschsen]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:qq]] }} [[sn:Sebschesen]] [[ja:セベシェン]] [[en:draft:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] [[sn:Sebeschsen]] {{citation needed|Sebeschsen}} [[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sebschesen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sebschesen]][[Category:qq]] [[sn: failed]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Sebschesen]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:Pages with German translation box]] {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}}

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=draft}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|de|Heliakisch|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Heliacal rising|qq=}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|de|Sesostris III.|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Senusret III|qq=}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|de|12. Dynastie|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|qq=}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|fr|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|de|Christian Leitz|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Christian Leitz|qq=}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. ''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|de|Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. ''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. revision.comment: Text replacement - "Ancient Egyptian Decan Stars" to "Ancient Egyptian Decans"
revision.text:
{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2263469]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = =S29-D58 N33*N33:V13 N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME2 = =E17-D58 V13:O34 N14 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=}} |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Sebeches
''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:セベシェン]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q2263469}}

{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[d:Q2263469]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = =S29-D58 N33*N33:V13 N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME2 = =E17-D58 V13:O34 N14 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=}} |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Sebeches
''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[Category:completion tier 1]] {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]][[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]] }} [[ja:セベシェン]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:pages without english wikidata name]] {{wikidata link|Q2263469}} [[Category:Pages linked to by wikidata]] [[Category:Matching pagename on wikidata]]

''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. ''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=|WD=Q319775}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=|WD=Q192322}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. ''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=|WD=Q319775}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=|WD=Q192322}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. [[de:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2263469]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = =S29-D58 N33*N33:V13 N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = [[:en:Middle Kingdom of Egypt|Middle Kingdom of Egypt]] |NAME2 = =E17-D58 V13:O34 N14 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=|WD=Q105747718}} |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Sebeches
''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=|WD=Q319775}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=|WD=Q192322}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:セベシェン]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{wikidata link|Q2263469}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an [[:en:Ancient Egypt|Ancient Egypt]] [[:en:Decan|Decan]], which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and was part of the [[:en:Constellation|Constellation]] {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=|WD=Q319775}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is [[:en:Spica|Spica]] in the constellation [[:en:Virgo (constellation)|Virgo]], which was also regarded as the [[:en:Star|Star]] of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=|WD=Q192322}} and was celebrated during the annual [[:en:Min_festival|Min festival]] processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the [[:en:Heliacal rising|Heliacal rising]] was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under [[:en:Senusret III|Senusret III]] ([[:en:Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt]]) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * [[:en:Christian Leitz|Christian Leitz]]: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}} {{draft categories| }} [[ja:セベシェン]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{wikidata link|Q2263469}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:セベシェン]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{wikidata link|Q2263469}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

[[de:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q2263469]] Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 20:24, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 05:26, 12 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{afc comment|Wikidata entry (none) Wikidata name: (none) [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:08, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = =S29-D58 N33*N33:V13 N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME2 = =E17-D58 V13:O34 N14 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=|WD=Q105747718}} |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Sebeches
''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=Constellation}} {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=|WD=Q319775}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|en|Spica|WD=Q13008|lt=Spica}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|en|Virgo (constellation)|WD=Q8842|lt=Virgo}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=|WD=Q192322}} and was celebrated during the annual {{ill|Min_festival|en|Min_festival|WD=Q1936130|lt=Min festival}} processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:セベシェン]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{wikidata link|Q2263469}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:qq]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

[[de:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{Infobox hieroglyphs two-columns |NAME = =S29-D58 N33*N33:V13 N14 |NAME-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|en|Middle Kingdom of Egypt|WD=Q191324|lt=Middle Kingdom of Egypt}} |NAME2 = =E17-D58 V13:O34 N14 |NAME2-ERWEITERT = {{ill|Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|de|Ägypten in griechisch-römischer Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Egypt in the Greco-Roman Period|qq=|WD=Q105747718}} |NAME2-ERKLÄRUNG = Sebeches
''Sbẖs''
read as ''Sebeschsen''
''The Encompassing One'' }} '''Sebeschsen''' (also '''Sebeches''') is the designation of an {{ill|Ancient Egypt|en|Ancient Egypt|WD=Q11768|lt=Ancient Egypt}} {{ill|Decan|en|Decan|WD=Q5248738|lt=Decan}}, which comprised at least two {{ill|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|de|Baktiu|1=Baktiu|2=de|3=Baktiu|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Baktiu|qq=|WD=Q804527}} (decan stars) and was part of the {{ill|Constellation|en|Constellation|WD=Q8928|lt=Constellation}} {{ill|Abesches|de|Abesches|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Abesches|qq=|WD=Q319775}}. The most prominent decan star associated with Sebeschsen is {{ill|Spica|en|Spica|WD=Q13008|lt=Spica}} in the constellation {{ill|Virgo (constellation)|en|Virgo (constellation)|WD=Q8842|lt=Virgo}}, which was also regarded as the {{ill|Star|en|Star|WD=Q523|lt=Star}} of the deity {{ill|Min (Egyptian mythology)|de|Min (Ägyptische Mythologie)|lt=Min|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Min (Egyptian mythology)|qq=|WD=Q192322}} and was celebrated during the annual {{ill|Min_festival|en|Min_festival|WD=Q1936130|lt=Min festival}} processions. In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''. Peeters, Leuven 2002, ISBN 2-87723-649-8, pp. 253–256. * {{ill|Alexandra von Lieven|fr|Alexandra von Lieven|de|Alexandra von Lieven|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Alexandra von Lieven|qq=|WD=Q2643704}}: ''Der Himmel über Esna – Eine Fallstudie zur religiösen Astronomie in Ägypten am Beispiel der kosmologischen Decken- und Architravinschriften im Tempel von Esna''. Harrassowitz, Wiesbaden 2000, ISBN 3-447-04324-5, pp. 174–175. * Alexandra von Lieven: ''Grundriss des Laufes der Sterne – Das sogenannte Nutbuch''. The Carsten Niebuhr Institute of Ancient Eastern Studies (et al.), Copenhagen 2007, ISBN 978-87-635-0406-5, pp. 62–67 and 385. {{translated page|de|Sebeschsen|version=186404311|comment=Imported full de history}} [[ja:セベシェン]] [[de:Sebeschsen]] {{wikidata link|Q2263469}} [[Category:Ancient Egyptian Decans]] [[Category:completion tier 1]] [[Category:Wikidata has short description]] [[Category:WikiProject Ancient Egypt]]

In the decan lists of the {{ill|Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|lt=Pharaonic period|de|Nutbuch#Pharaonische Zeit|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Book of Nut#Pharaonic period|qq=|WD=Q2005559|1=Book of Nut}}, Sebeschsen represented the eighth decan on the body of {{ill|Nut (Egyptian mythology)|de|Nut (Ägyptische Mythologie)|1=Nut (goddess)|lt=Nut|lt=Nut|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (Egyptian mythology)|13=User:Immanuelle/Nut (goddess)|qq=|WD=Q165496}}, which was divided into two sub-decans. The {{ill|Acronychic|de|akronych|Acronychic=Acronychic|lt=Acronychal rising|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Acronychic|qq=|WD=Q4059812|1=Acronychic}} or "entry into the underworld" was set for the {{ill|Season of the Harvest|lt=6th Schemu I|de|6. Schemu I|Season of the Harvest=Season of the Harvest|lt_2=Shemu#Dating|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Season of the Harvest|qq=|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work|comment=no wikipedias work}}, and the {{ill|Heliacal rising|en|Heliacal rising|WD=Q368373|lt=Heliacal rising|1=Heliacal rising}} was set for the 16th Schemu III, based on the arrangement ordered under {{ill|Senusret III|en|Senusret III|WD=Q19248|lt=Senusret III|1=Senusret III}} ({{ill|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|en|Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|WD=Q719639|lt=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt|1=Twelfth Dynasty of Egypt}}) in his seventh year of reign. == Literature == * {{ill|Christian Leitz|en|Christian Leitz|WD=Q1080802|lt=Christian Leitz|1=Christian Leitz}}: ''Altägyptische Sternuhren''. Peeters, Leuven 1995, ISBN 90-6831-669-9, pp. 88–89. * Christian Leitz et al.: ''{{ill|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|de|Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen|qq=|WD=Q979923|1=Lexikon der ägyptischen Götter und Götterbezeichnungen}}; Bd. 6 H̱ - s''.
